seoTitle: Riverwood vs Rob Roy — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Riverwood and Rob Roy. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Riverwood vs Rob Roy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Riverwood and Rob Roy.
heading: Riverwood vs Rob Roy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Riverwood, New South Wales and Rob Roy, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rob Roy has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Riverwood (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Riverwood or Rob Roy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Riverwood has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Rob Roy scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Riverwood scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Rob Roy scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Riverwood or Rob Roy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rob Roy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
